questions: biomedical engineering te ki shekha jai?
answers: Biomedical engineering applies engineering principles to medicine and biology. It involves designing medical devices, prosthetics, imaging equipment, and developing new therapies.
Biomedical engineering holo engineering ar medicine er moddhe bridge. Era artificial organs, MRI machines, surgical robots er moto jinish design kore.
Biomedical engineers develop technologies like pacemakers, artificial joints, imaging systems (MRI, CT scan), and drug delivery systems to improve healthcare.
Biomedical engineering combines biology, medicine, and engineering to create solutions for healthcare. From prosthetic limbs to genetic engineering tools, it's a rapidly growing field.
